response.overview: A strange legend says that the Sun of ancient Peru, contrary to what history and tradition maintain, was not a source of life and fertility, but of destruction and death. This led to the ideas of the apocalypse and the end of history being embodied in episodes that occurred at various points in Peruvian history. In the past, a young woman from the viceregal aristocracy falls in love with a leper. In the present, drug addiction spreads, with the consequence of multiple social tensions. In the future, the fear of nuclear holocaust moves a character, Dr. Olmar, to warn humanity of the impending risk.
response.title: El Sol de los Muertos
